PM Borne faces challenge of keeping the left in check in the legislative elections                Unlike her two predecessors, France's new prime minister is expected to embody social liberalism. Being integrated into the left should allow the head of state's party to respond to the threat represented by the leftwing coalition.

Subscribers onlyBritish government threatens to dismantle Northern Ireland protocol  London is challenging parts of the Brexit treaty that establish a customs border between Northern Ireland and the rest of the United Kingdom.

Baby formula shortage: Biden invokes Defense Production Act to speed up manufacturing  Facing political pressure over a domestic shortage of baby formula, U.S. President also authorized the Defense Department to use commercial aircraft to fly supplies from overseas.
Cannes Film Festival: Tom Cruise awarded honorary Palme d'Or  The French Air Force flew over the red carpet as 'Top Gun: Maverick' the sequel to the 1986 blockbuster premiered.

News   Thomas Saintourens Subscribers onlyNo more escape for a Camorra mobster-turned-chef Antonio Cuozzo Nasti was sentenced to 16 years in prison in for theft, possession of stolen goods, carrying an illegal weapon and violating a special surveillance order. He fled Italy eight years ago and was arrested in France in early May.   4 min read

Letter from...   Cécile Ducourtieux Subscribers onlyThe British are moved by Deborah James' courage in the face of terminal illness Diagnosed with terminal bowel cancer, the young woman has been chronicling her battle against the disease for five years, and advocating for prevention. Her recently launched donation campaign to help medical research has raised more than 7 million euros in just a few days.   4 min read

In Depth   Cédric Pietralunga Subscribers only'The Donbas today is the Somme of 1916': Ukraine's battle of the shells As the conflict enters a more static phase, in which each side attempts to inflict maximum damage on the other, artillery is playing a central role.   4 min read
